models.objective: Quantify how much a player's passes progress play toward the opponent goal.
assumptions: Field coordinates are normalized and consistent.
Progress toward goal correlates with threat creation.
Role normalization captures baseline expectations.
risk_conditions: Teams with extreme styles (direct play) inflate raw progression.
Backpasses under press may be undervalued despite stabilizing possession.
models.counter_scenarios: A team uses wide circulation then a single vertical pass; PPS may over-credit the final passer while under-crediting movers and prior pass chains.

models.objective: Estimate off-ball contributions via passing-network centrality and spatial influence.
assumptions: Passing network is a valid proxy for involvement/space-creation.
Centrality relates to tactical influence.
risk_conditions: Certain roles (decoy runners) contribute without network visibility.
models.counter_scenarios: A forward makes repeated decoy runs creating space but receives few passes; OBIS underestimates without tracking-based spatial features.

models.objective: Detect and interpret outliers in player metric distributions with robust statistics and clustering.
assumptions: Underlying distribution is stable within role/league.
Outliers may represent skill or noise.
models.counter_scenarios: A player appears as an outlier due to tactical role (e.g., set-piece target) rather than transferable skill; validating with role + video breaks the inference.

models.objective: Separate finishing performance by shot type (feet, open-play headers, set-piece headers) to reduce mixing bias.
assumptions: Shot-type segments reflect distinct skills.
xG is calibrated within segment.
risk_conditions: Team cross volume biases opportunity set.
models.counter_scenarios: A striker's 'poor finishing' overall is driven by low-xG volley attempts; segmenting by shot-type and location changes conclusion.

models.objective: Explain conceptual and practical differences between xT (state value) and VAEP (probability delta) and why rankings differ.
assumptions: Event data sufficiently captures action context.
Model design choices are explicit (labeling, horizons, action set).
models.counter_scenarios: A possession-heavy 6 has many low-risk passes; xT may reward territory gain, VAEP may reward probability deltas—rankings invert depending on objective.

models.objective: Separate intention vs execution in VAEP and model player value as a time series (volatility, development curves).
assumptions: Feature split meaningfully isolates intent vs execution.
Time indexing (date or matches) is consistent.
models.counter_scenarios: A player's surge is driven by role change into higher-value zones; time-series without role conditioning overstates true improvement.

models.objective: Contextualize physical runs (HI runs) using tactical layers and map to value creation via EPV.
assumptions: Broadcast tracking is accurate enough.
Dynamic lines/block modeling approximates defensive structure.
models.counter_scenarios: A team presses with short bursts <21 km/h but high acceleration; HI threshold misses key efforts—use acceleration-based alternatives.

models.model_name: Pitch ownership / pitch control via KNN
models.objective: Build flexible pitch control surfaces using KNN with uncertainty parameters to emulate multiple definitions.
assumptions: Nearest-neighbor structure approximates reachability/control.
Hyperparameters capture uncertainty across skill levels.
risk_conditions: No quantitative ground truth for control surfaces.
Ball speed/physics ignored can misestimate intercepts.
models.counter_scenarios: Aerial passes: ground-control model says receiver controls but ball flight time enables interception; integrate ball travel time or passable region.

models.objective: Model safe ball progression as transitions between 'safe configurations' (passing cliques) using pitch control + passing lanes.
assumptions: Having multiple open options increases progression robustness.
Passing lanes within passable region represent 'safe' options.
risk_conditions: Physics parameters for ball speed/deceleration uncertain.
Clique criterion may be too strict and miss viable progressions.
models.counter_scenarios: A team plays third-man combinations where direct clique is rare; model undercounts success patterns unless clique size/horizon adjusted.

models.objective: Automatically detect recurring pass sequences from ball trajectory event sequences using Dynamic Time Warping.
assumptions: Recurring sequences reflect trained tactical routines.
Trajectory normalization aligns comparable patterns.
risk_conditions: DTW computational cost on massive logs.
Different opponents force shape changes that break pattern matching.
models.counter_scenarios: A pattern appears frequent due to common constraints (sideline exits) not strategy; validating with contextual labels removes false 'tactics'.

models.model_name: Formation classification in single match situations (FSIM)
models.objective: Classify formations for short match situations via template similarity and visualize formation summaries.
assumptions: Templates represent meaningful tactical archetypes.
Short segments can be summarized reliably.
risk_conditions: Different interpretations of roles yield low inter-annotator agreement.
models.counter_scenarios: Two schemes differ only by one player's role; numerical label (4-4-2 vs 4-2-3-1) is misleading—use role embeddings instead.

models.model_name: Tactical situations & playing styles as KPIs (GEE)
models.objective: Test which tactical situations/styles correlate with match outcome using a GEE logistic framework across leagues.
assumptions: Tactical situation coding is consistent across leagues.
Associations are stable across contexts.
models.counter_scenarios: A team leads early then defends deeper; style-outcome correlation reflects game-state, not causal superiority—control for scoreline/time.
